name: eza (Modern ls Replacement)
pricing_notes: Open source (MIT); eza-community; fork of exa

performance.notes: Rust-based; slightly slower than ls due to features; negligible in practice

agent_readiness.notes: Modern ls replacement. Colors by file type. Icons (Nerd Fonts). Git status per file. --long (-l), --tree (-T), --all (-a), --grid. --git for git status column. --icons. Hyperlinks. File size formatting. -snm sort modes. Group directories first. Header row. Fork of archived exa project. Actively maintained. Standard in modern developer dotfiles. alias ls='eza --icons --group-directories-first' pattern. Rust-based (same ecosystem as bat, fd, ripgrep).

gotchas: CLI tool only — not a library
Fork of abandoned exa — verify ongoing maintenance
best_when: Developer workstations wanting enhanced directory listings
Terminal setups with Nerd Fonts wanting file type icons
Git repos where per-file git status in ls output is useful
Tree view for project structure exploration
avoid_when: Shell scripts requiring POSIX ls compatibility
summary: eza is a modern ls replacement with colors, Nerd Font icons, git status per file, and tree view. Rust-based fork of the archived exa project. --long/-l, --tree/-T, --git, --icons. Standard in developer dotfiles as ls alias. Best for developer workstations with Nerd Fonts wanting enhanced directory listings with git awareness.
